memberdef.argsstring: (const Vector4< T > &x, const Vector4< T > &y, const Vector4< T > &z, const Vector4< T > &w)
memberdef.name: Matrix4

briefdescription.para: construct from rotation matrix and translation vector

detaileddescription.para: Currently AffineXf3<long long> doesn't seem to compile, so we disable this constructor for Matrix4<long long>, because otherwise mrbind instantiates the entire AffineXf3<long long> and chokes on it.

memberdef.argsstring: (!std::is_same_v< T, U >) const expr explicit Matrix4(const Matrix4< U > &m)
memberdef.name: MR_REQUIRES_IF_SUPPORTED

detaileddescription.para: Here T == U doesn't seem to cause any issues in the C++ code, but we're still disabling it because it somehow gets emitted when generating the bindings, and results in duplicate functions in C#.

briefdescription.para: compute sum of squared matrix elements

inbodydescription.para: Calling sqrt this way to hopefully support boost.multiprecision numbers. Returning auto to not break on integral types.

briefdescription.para: computes submatrix of the matrix with excluded i-th row and j-th column

memberdef.type: Matrix4< T > inverse() const noexcept MR_REQUIRES_IF_SUPPORTED(!std constexpr Matrix4< T >
memberdef.definition: Matrix4< T > inverse() const noexcept MR_REQUIRES_IF_SUPPORTED(!std constexpr Matrix4< T > MR::Matrix4< T >::transposed
memberdef.name: transposed

briefdescription.para: returns a matrix that scales uniformly

memberdef.type: Vector3< T > operator()(const Vector3< T > &b) const MR_REQUIRES_IF_SUPPORTED(!std friend constexpr bool
memberdef.definition: Vector3< T > operator()(const Vector3< T > &b) const MR_REQUIRES_IF_SUPPORTED(!std friend constexpr bool operator==
memberdef.name: operator==

detaileddescription.para: converts 3d-vector b in 4d-vector (b,1), multiplies matrix on it, and assuming the result is in homogeneous coordinates returns it as 3d-vector

briefdescription.para: NOTE: We use std::declval() in the operators below because libclang 18 in our binding generator is bugged and chokes on decltyping a.x and such. TODO fix this when we update libclang.

memberdef.definition: Matrix4< decltype(std::declval< T >() - std::declval< T >())> operator-
memberdef.name: operator-

memberdef.type: Vector4< decltype(dot(std::declval< Vector4< T > >(), std::declval< Vector4< T > >()))>
memberdef.definition: Vector4< decltype(dot(std::declval< Vector4< T > >(), std::declval< Vector4< T > >()))> operator*
memberdef.name: operator*

memberdef.type: Matrix4< decltype(dot(std::declval< Vector4< T > >(), std::declval< Vector4< T > >()))>
memberdef.definition: Matrix4< decltype(dot(std::declval< Vector4< T > >(), std::declval< Vector4< T > >()))> operator*
memberdef.name: operator*
